videos.title: Recognizing Shapes in Our World | Geometry for Kids
videos.description: Look at the world around you and identify shapes everywhere! See circles in wheels and clocks, rectangles in doors and books, triangles in roofs and sandwiches, and 3D shapes in everyday objects like balls, boxes, and cans.
learningObjectives: Identify 2D and 3D shapes in everyday surroundings
Connect geometry knowledge to the real world
keyConceptsCovered: 3D shapes in balls, boxes, cans, cones
followUpTopics: Views of objects from different positions

videos.title: Shape Scavenger Hunt | Finding Shapes at Home and School
videos.description: Go on a shape scavenger hunt! Find shapes in your kitchen, bedroom, classroom, and playground. See how many shapes you can spot in everyday life.
learningObjectives: Name both 2D and 3D shapes in objects
keyConceptsCovered: Kitchen shapes: cylinder cans, rectangular boxes
Classroom shapes: rectangular books, circular clocks
Playground shapes: spherical balls, cylindrical poles

videos.title: Shapes in Art and Architecture | Geometry Is Beautiful
videos.description: Discover how shapes make beautiful art and buildings! See circles in arches, triangles in pyramids, rectangles in skyscrapers, and hexagons in beehives. Geometry is everywhere in nature and human creations.
learningObjectives: Recognize shapes in art and architecture
Appreciate geometry in nature and design

videos.title: Shape Detective Game | Can You Find All the Shapes?
videos.description: Be a shape detective! Look at pictures of everyday scenes and find as many shapes as you can. How many circles, rectangles, triangles, and 3D shapes can you spot?
learningObjectives: Spot shapes quickly in complex scenes
Apply all shape knowledge in context
keyConceptsCovered: Finding multiple shapes in one scene

videos.title: Create Shape Art! | Drawing with 2D and 3D Shapes
videos.description: Use your shape knowledge to create art! Draw a house using rectangles, triangles, and squares. Make a robot using circles and rectangles. See how shapes combine to make amazing pictures.
learningObjectives: Use shapes creatively to make drawings
Combine different shapes to create pictures
keyConceptsCovered: House = rectangle + triangle roof + square windows

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through shape identification with art and architecture focus

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of identifying shapes with scavenger hunt and creative activities

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core shape identification concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: A wheel on a bicycle is shaped like a:
questions.explanation: A bicycle wheel is shaped like a circle. It needs to be round so it can roll smoothly!

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Take students on a 'shape walk' around the school. Give each student a checklist of shapes to find. Have them draw the objects they find and label the shapes.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: During a walk or car ride, play 'I Spy' with shapes. Say 'I spy something shaped like a cylinder' and let your child find it. Grocery stores and parks are great for this!
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Become a shape detective! Look around your room, kitchen, or school and try to find at least 3 objects for each shape: circle, rectangle, triangle, square, sphere, cube, cylinder, and cone.
